name: Texas Barbecue Beef Brisket
recipeIngredient: Oak, pecan, mesquite, or hickory chips - soaked 30 minutes
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare briquets and add desired wood chips.
2. Trim excess fat from beef brisket.
3. Combine paprika and half a teaspoon of black pepper; rub evenly over the surface of the brisket.
4. Place brisket, fat side down, in an 11 1/2 x 9-inch disposable aluminum pan.
5. Add one cup of water to the pan.
6. Cover pan tightly with aluminum foil.
7. Place the pan in the center of the grill over very low coals.
8. Cook for 5 hours, turning brisket over every 1 1/2 hours.
9. Remove excess fat from the pan as it accumulates using a baster; add additional water to the pan if needed.
10. Periodically add just enough additional briquets to keep the coals at very low temperature.
11. Remove brisket from the pan and reserve the pan drippings.
12. Place brisket, fat side down, directly over very low coals on the grill.
13. Replace the grill cover and continue cooking for 30 minutes.
14. Skim and discard fat from the reserved pan drippings; set aside one cup of drippings.
15. Melt butter in a medium saucepan over medium heat.
16. Add onions and cook until tender-crisp, stirring occasionally.
17. Add reserved pan drippings, remaining half teaspoon of black pepper, catsup, lemon juice, worcestershire sauce, and hot pepper sauce; simmer for approximately 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
18. Trim excess fat from brisket; carve across the grain into thin slices.
